'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:CategoryCategoryk]] [[Category:CategoryCategory]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Category]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Category]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:Category]] [[ja:塚前古墳]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[ja:塚前古墳]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:塚前古墳]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:New]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:New]]

Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:New]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:New]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Tumulus Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqqq]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Tumulus?" ["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Sqq]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

'''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|ja|東北地方}}, it ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijin-yama Kofun|ja|雷神山古墳}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|ja|遠見塚古墳}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|ja|稲荷森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:s11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:sukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]][[Category:11]]
