{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|qq=|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|qq=|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tsukamae Kofun]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[sn:sukamae Kofun]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|qq=|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|qq=|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|qq=|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|qq=|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}}

Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]

Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] to [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}}

[[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]

Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}}

A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.text:
[[ja:塚前古墳]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "pages with conflicting qids" to "qq"
revision.text:
[[ja:塚前古墳]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. Its shape is that of a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]], it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # [[:en:Raijinyama Kofun|Raijin-yama Kofun]] (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # [[:en:Inarimori Kofun|Inarimori Kofun]] (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[ja:塚前古墳]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |image=Tsukamae_burial_mound_Iwaki_Fukushima.jpg |coordinates={{Coord|36.985433|N|140.895483|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48753149]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukamae Kofun''' (塚前古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Onahama Rinjo|ja|小名浜林城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onahama Rinjo|1=Onahama Rinjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128847002}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. Its shape is that of a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}. It is not designated as a historic site. Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}}, it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijinyama Kofun|en|Raijinyama Kofun|WD=Q11660187|lt=Raijin-yama Kofun}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|en|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710|lt=Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Ōtsukayama Kofun|WD=Q11381233|lt=Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|en|Inarimori Kofun|WD=Q11596873|lt=Inarimori Kofun}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Among kofun in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}}, it Ranks 3rd to 8th in sizeMajor kofun in the Tōhoku region include: # {{ill|Raijinyama Kofun|en|Raijinyama Kofun|WD=Q11660187|lt=Raijin-yama Kofun}} (Natori, Miyagi) - 168 meters in length. # {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|en|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710|lt=Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} (Aizubange, Fukushima) - 129 meters in length. # {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Ōtsukayama Kofun|WD=Q11381233|lt=Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun}} (Aizuwakamatsu, Fukushima) - 114 meters in length. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. # {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun}} (Sendai, Miyagi) - 110 meters in length. # {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Ōsaki, Miyagi) - 100 meters in length. # {{ill|Inarimori Kofun|en|Inarimori Kofun|WD=Q11596873|lt=Inarimori Kofun}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - 96 meters in length. Recent surveys suggest that other kofun, including Tsukamae Kofun (95–120 meters in length), may also be classified as large-scale tumuli: * {{ill|Minamimori Kofun|ja|南森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamimori Kofun|1=Minamimori Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532891}} (Nanyō, Yamagata) - Estimated to be 150–168 meters in length. and is the largest kofun from the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} in the Tōhoku region, dated to the mid-6th century. == Overview == Tsukamae Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located in the eastern part of Fukushima Prefecture, in the Hamadōri area, at the head of Onahama Bay on the alluvial plain of the right bank of the Yada River (a tributary of the Fujiwara River). The mound has been significantly leveled and eroded over time.{{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} Excavations conducted by the Iwaki City Board of Education in fiscal 2016 (Heisei 28) and a survey by Fukushima University revealed its scale and construction method.{{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} The tumulus, now irregularly shaped, is reconstructed as originally being keyhole-shaped, with the front portion oriented southwest. Its mound measures 95–120 meters in length, Ranking 3rd to 8th in size among Tōhoku kofun and is the largest from the late Kofun period in the region. Remains of haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa (some morning glory-shaped) and figurative haniwa (lid and shield-shaped), have been found on the mound surface. A surrounding moat (likely horseshoe-shaped) has also been identified. While the burial facilities are unknown, past records suggest the presence of ornaments such as gyōba (杏葉). Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
["Tsukamae Kofun: Measuring 120m, Largest Late Tōhoku Kofun?" (Kahoku Shimpō, May 11, 2017)]. == Kofun Details == Measurements from the 2017 survey are as follows: * Mound length: 95–120 meters * Rear round portion: Diameter 53 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Length: 54–70 meters ** Width: 45–65 meters The mound was constructed by alternating layers of earth to form embankments, then filling the inner area, a technique characteristic of western Japan. Additionally, soil bags and clod stacking were used, a method typically reserved for high-status tombs in the late Kofun period. == Notes == {{Notelist|group="note"}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Harvnb|塚前古墳 調査概報|2017}} * {{Harvnb|福島大学 測量調査|2017}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚前古墳|version=99995314|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚前古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48753149}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Based on the haniwa and other artifacts, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the mid-6th century, making it significant for studying the late Kofun period in eastern Japan. == Site History == * '''2016 (Heisei 28)''': Excavation due to residential development. Confirmed as a large-scale kofun (Iwaki City Board of Education). * '''2017 (Heisei 29)''': Survey conducted. Confirmed as the largest late Kofun in Tōhoku (Fukushima University Archaeological Research Laboratory).["Iwaki's Tsukamae Kofun: Largest Late Kofun in Tōhoku Revealed by Survey" (Mainichi Shimbun, May 11, 2017)]
